title: 📋 Host Worksheet (template)
body: Per-target working sheet: live summary up top, a pre-seeded per-port To-Try list, loot tables, and proof. Hit the obsidian button to copy it verbatim.
cmd: # obsidian button copies the template below as-is.
warn: Pastes verbatim (no technique scaffolding) because this is a template node.
markdown:
---
type: host
ip:
hostname:
os:
role:
status: enumerating
---

# <hostname>  ·  <ip>

> **NOW:**  ← overwrite this constantly. What am I trying *right this second*?
> **NEXT:**  ← the one thing to do if NOW dies.

| | |
|---|---|
| **IP** |  |
| **Hostname / Domain** |  |
| **OS / Build** |  |
| **Role** | (web · DC · file · DB · workstation) |
| **Foothold?** | ☐ |  **Root/Admin?** ☐ |

---

## To-Try List

> One block per open port. Marker shows state at a glance:
> `[!]` hot lead · `[~]` not tried yet · `[?]` needs creds/info first · `[x]` dead end · `[✓]` done
> The arrows are pre-seeded first moves — delete what doesn't apply, add what you find.

**[~] 80 / 443  HTTP(S)**
→ whatweb / curl -I (server, framework, headers)
→ ferox/dirb dirs, then files (.php .txt .bak .old .zip .git)
→ view-source + robots.txt + sitemap + JS for endpoints/comments
→ default creds on any login; version → searchsploit
→ params (?id ?file ?page) → SQLi / LFI / cmd-injection
→ vhost/subdomain fuzz if you have a hostname

**[?] 22  SSH**
→ no first move — SSH is a DOOR, not a key. Need a user+pass/key from elsewhere.
→ version → searchsploit (rarely; e.g. user-enum CVE-2018-15473)
→ once you have creds: ssh user@ip ; try key from loot

**[~] 445 / 139  SMB**
→ nxc smb <ip>  (signing, OS, domain, null-auth)
→ null/guest: nxc smb <ip> -u '' -p '' --shares ; smbclient -N -L //<ip>
→ version → MS17-010 (EternalBlue) check
→ with creds: --shares, spider, --sam/--lsa if admin

**[~] 21  FTP**
→ anonymous:anonymous ; ls for read, try upload for write
→ version → searchsploit ; can you write to a web-served dir?

**[?] 3389  RDP**
→ needs creds. nxc rdp <ip> -u .. -p ..  (Pwn3d! = admin)
→ xfreerdp /u: /p: /v:<ip>  once you have them

**[?] 5985  WinRM**
→ needs creds. nxc winrm <ip> -u .. -p ..
→ evil-winrm -i <ip> -u .. -p ..  (or -H <hash>)

**[~] 3306 / 5432 / 1433  DB (MySQL/Postgres/MSSQL)**
→ default/weak creds ; version → searchsploit
→ MSSQL: xp_cmdshell if sa ; Postgres: COPY/large-object RCE
→ creds reuse from other hosts/loot

**[~] <port>  <service>**
→ banner-grab: nc <ip> <port> / curl
→ version → searchsploit ; unknown → research the fingerprint
